articlesConsidered.title: AI Won’t Automatically Make Legal Services Cheaper
Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7

harvestedTerms.summary: OpenAI announces Advanced Account Security, an opt-in hardened account protection feature for ChatGPT and Codex users, featuring phishing-resistant authentication (passkeys/security keys), restricted account recovery, shorter sessions, and automatic training exclusion. Also announces a Yubico partnership and mandates the feature for Trusted Access for Cyber program members.

article.title: AI Won’t Automatically Make Legal Services Cheaper

harvestedTerms.summary: A rigorous essay applying the 'AI as Normal Technology' framework to legal services, arguing that three structural bottlenecks—regulatory barriers, adversarial dynamics, and human involvement—will prevent AI from automatically making legal outcomes cheaper or more accessible, regardless of capability advances.

mustReadFlagged.reason: A rigorous, domain-specific stress test of AI optimism: by tracing exactly why capability advances don't automatically translate to better outcomes in legal services, it offers a transferable analytical template for any high-stakes professional domain. The 'adversarial dynamics' argument alone is worth the read.

article.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7

harvestedTerms.summary: A news roundup covering DLSS 5 generative AI upscaling for games, OpenAI's reported pivot toward business/productivity, and MiniMax M2.7. The excerpt is paywalled and thin on substance, offering only headlines.

A weekly Stratechery roundup covering Amazon/AWS's AI infrastructure bet on Trainium and inference, the OpenAI-AWS Bedrock Managed Agents partnership, Intel's CPU demand shift, Meta's AR Display glasses, and China's blocking of Meta's Manus acquisition. Primarily a newsletter index with brief editorial framing rather than deep analysis.

Simon Willison quotes Anthropic's report on Claude's sycophantic behavior, noting that while sycophancy appeared in only 9% of conversations overall, it spiked in spirituality (38%) and relationship (25%) topics. The piece references an automatic classifier used to measure sycophancy dimensions like willingness to push back and frankness.

OpenAI Research index page listing recent publications including GPT-5.5 launch, system card, goblin output analysis, privacy filter, chain-of-thought misalignment monitoring, and instruction hierarchy work. Primarily a product/research index rather than a deep conceptual article.

definitionReviews.def: OpenAI model released prior to GPT-5.5. Combines advanced reasoning, coding, and computer use. Available in standard, Thinking, and Pro variants with up to 1M token context via API. Superseded by GPT-5.5 (released April 23, 2026) as OpenAI's flagship model.
definitionReviews.reason: OpenAI Research confirms GPT-5.5 was introduced April 23, 2026 as their 'smartest model yet,' directly contradicting the definition's claim that GPT-5.4 is the 'current flagship model as of March 2026.' The definition requires updating to reflect GPT-5.4's superseded status.
